images.caption: Figure 1:Dominoencodes domain characteristics into latent embeddings derived from reference samples, which are then used to guide LLMs in generating new samples. While the domain characteristics are not explicitly defined in natural language, a comparison between reference and synthetic samples at least reveals a shared structural pattern (e.g., description, examples, constraints), even though the specific content differs, highlighting theDomino’s ability to capture the domain’s distribution—at least its visible format.

Figure 2:The left side represents the minimal sufficient domain representation learning process, wheret1,⋯,tTt_{1},\cdots,t_{T}denote the raw natural language tokens of a reference sample,eme_{m}denotes the corresponding embedding. Domain-level soft tokens𝑫∗{\bm{D}}^{*}and sample-level soft tokens𝑺(i){\bm{S}}^{(i)}are jointly optimized. The right side represents using only optimized𝑫∗{\bm{D}}^{*}to synthesize domain-specific samples.
